competitors.aiSummary:
# Kamino Summary

Kamino expanded lending incentives across multiple markets: OnRefinance increased to $25K monthly rewards; Solstice Market exceeded $50M TVL with Season 2 Flares launched; xStocks Market now accepts USDC collateral for tokenized equities (SPYx, TSLAx, QQQx, NVDAx); Rockaway RWA Vault surpassed $34M deposits, generating 5.61% APY.

tweets.fullText:
Monthly incentives for the @onrefinance Market have increased to $25,000.

• $18,750 in monthly USDC supply rewards
• $6,250 in monthly USDC borrow rewards

Earn reinsurance-backed yield and stack rewards on Kamino. https://t.co/B9Xg0MHbVf
tweets.aiAnalysis: The tweet underperformed due to lack of emotional urgency or compelling differentiation—while the $25k incentive increase provides specificity, it reads as a routine incentive announcement without clear context on why this matters (is this a significant increase? how does it compare?) or what makes "reinsurance-backed yield" meaningfully better than alternatives. The image likely just shows static numbers rather than performance data or visual proof of returns, which would have driven more engagement.

tweets.fullText:
Season 2 Flares are live on the Solstice Market.

Supply eUSX, USX, or USDG to earn Flares or borrow against Exponent PT collateral: PT-USX and PT-eUSX maturing June 2026 and eUSX.

Onchain credit markets continue to scale on Kamino. https://t.co/a5d5CRhCCy
tweets.aiAnalysis:
# Analysis

The low engagement (1.5% engagement rate) stems from **product announcement format without emotional stakes or specificity**. The tweet reads as a feature launch notification rather than a value proposition—it lists mechanics (supply/borrow/earn) and token tickers without clarifying *why* users should care (yield rates, competitive advantages, or risk/reward specifics). The inclusion of multiple token pairs and technical jargon creates friction, while the lack of numbers (APY, TVL growth, comparative performance) removes the quantitative hook that drives DeFi engagement.

No urgency or controversy to drive debate-driven engagement.
